scenarios.title: Agent Frameworks, Tooling, and Sandboxed LLM Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: LLM agent frameworks, browser automation agents, tool calling, graph/state runtimes, hosted or sandbox execution, MCP, CLIs, and production agent operations.
scenarios.role: Use this skill for maintaining, migrating, debugging, and safely operating Microsoft AutoGen Python 0.7.x applications, including AgentChat, Core runtimes, extensions, Studio, Magentic-One, AG Bench, and pyautogen compatibility.
scenarios.read_when: The request names `autogen` or asks for package-specific APIs, CLIs, configs, data/model artifacts, error messages, workflows, or repository maintenance that match this project.
scenarios.best_for: agentchat workflows, core runtime, extensions integrations, and tools studio bench.
scenarios.avoid_when: another repo skill in this scenario matches the user's task, package, model family, data format, serving backend, or workflow more directly.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `autogen` when the request names `autogen`, centers on maintaining, migrating, debugging, and safely operating Microsoft AutoGen Python 0.7.x applications, including AgentChat, Core runtimes, extensions, Studio, Magentic-One, AG Bench, and pyautogen compatibility, uses its APIs or CLIs, references its configs/artifacts/errors, or asks for repository workflows in agent frameworks tooling and sandboxed llm workflows. Then route to agentchat workflows, core runtime, extensions integrations, or tools studio bench when those focused workflows match.
